descriptions: Joe Profaci, an American Mafia boss, helped mediate peace between the Greco factions.
edges.evidence_quote: Cottone ... still had good connections in the US, in particular with Joe Profaci, who came from the same village. At the time, Profaci was in Sicily and it seems he played an important role in the peace negotiations.

descriptions: The clans have both conflicts and cooperative agreements for drug trafficking and extortion.
edges.evidence_quote: Nonostante queste tre batterie siano state spesso in conflitto, non sono mancati accordi con i quali sono stati costituiti assetti multipartecipativi ad esempio del traffico di droga o delle estorsioni per condividere e spartire il profitti.

descriptions: The clans have both conflicts and cooperative agreements for drug trafficking and extortion.

descriptions: The Sinesi-Francavilla group and the Trisciuoglio-Prencipe group ended their war by forming an alliance to manage the funeral business together.
edges.evidence_quote: La guerra si concluse con un'alleanza tra i due gruppi consolidata al fine di gestire insieme il ricco business dei funerali.

descriptions: Clan Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese are close allies of Moretti-Pellegrino-Lanza.
edges.evidence_quote: i Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese, stretti alleati dei Moretti-Pellegrino .

descriptions: A hitman for Sinesi-Francavilla claimed that the boss of Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese was the actual perpetrator of a murder, implying a connection or rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo le dichiarazioni di Patrizio Villani, sicario garganico al soldo della batteria Sinesi-Francavilla e condannato a 30 anni di reclusione per l'omicidio Tizzano, l'autore dell'omicidio Panunzio fu in realtà Federico Trisciuoglio, boss dell'omonima batteria Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese e non Donato Delli Carri.

Bacon Brothers.description: The Bacon brothers are a trio of gangsters from Abbotsford, British Columbia, Canada, involved in drug trafficking and firearms offenses. They are implicated in multiple homicides in the Fraser Valley and Greater Vancouver area. The brothers rose from a middle-class background to become prominent figures in organized crime. Jonathan Bacon was murdered in 2011.

descriptions: The Bacon Brothers organization has links with the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: which is very influential in Western Canada, and has links with a notorious criminal organization

descriptions: The Bacon brothers partnered with the Red Scorpions and later took leadership control.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2006, the brothers partnered with the Red Scorpions gang to help them compete with the United Nations gang.

descriptions: Bacon Brothers were lured to align with Red Scorpions.
edges.evidence_quote: luring the Bacon Brothers away from the increasingly independent and unreliable UN to the more docile and malleable Red Scorpions

descriptions: Jarrod Bacon is identified as a member of both the Red Scorpions and the Bacon Brothers organization.
edges.evidence_quote: File information reveals that you are identified as a member of the Red Scorpions and the Bacon Brothers organization

descriptions: The Bacon brothers worked with the United Nations gang, but held them in contempt.
edges.evidence_quote: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) believed the Bacon brothers were working with the United Nations gang.

descriptions: Bacon Brothers were previously aligned with the United Nations gang.
edges.evidence_quote: luring the Bacon Brothers away from the increasingly independent and unreliable UN

descriptions: Jamie Bacon condemned the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Bacon condemned the Wolfpack Alliance together with the Indo-Canadian Brothers' Keepers gang in racist and homophobic language.

descriptions: Fresh Off the Boat Killers members were recruited to assassinate the Bacon brothers.
edges.evidence_quote: In early 2008, Clayton Roueche recruited two members of Calgary's Fresh Off the Boat Killers gang, namely Billy Ly and Troy Tran, to come to the Lower Mainland to kill the Bacon brothers.

descriptions: The Bacon brothers were members of the Red Scorpions gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Jamie did not resist arrest and was led into a police cruiser wearing his Red Scorpions hoodie and track pants.

descriptions: The UN gang, led by Tilli-Choli, actively hunted and attempted to kill the Bacon brothers.
edges.evidence_quote: Tilli-Choli, who became the most tenacious opponent of the Bacon brothers, was known for the 'human safaris' as he would spend hours cruising the Lower Mainland in his automobile looking for the Bacon brothers and other members of the Red Scorpions gang.

descriptions: Jonathan Bacon of the Bacon Brothers was a founding member of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Jonathan Bacon together with Larry Amero of the Hells Angels and James Riach and Randy Naicker of the Independent Soldiers formed a group called the Wolfpack Alliance in 2010.

Grape Street Watts Crips.description: The Grape Street Watts Crips is a set of the Crips gang based in the Watts neighborhood of Los Angeles, founded in the 1970s. It is known for a violent and long-lasting feud with the Bounty Hunter Bloods. The gang operates in California, Tennessee, and New Jersey. It remains active as of the present.

descriptions: They have a violent and long-lasting feud.
edges.evidence_quote: its longstanding rivalry with the Grape Street Watts Crips

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips is listed as a rival of Florencia 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 18th Street gang , 38th Street gang , Grape Street Watts Crips , Watts Varrio Grape Street 13 , Hat Gang Watts Crips , Huntington Park Locos , Playboys , East Side 13 , South Los 13

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips is a rival of Fruit Town Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Grape Street Watts Crips

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips and Bounty Hunter Bloods have a violent, long-lasting feud.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang's rivalry with the Bounty Hunter Bloods has been described as being 'the most violent and long lasting feud between two gangs that are in the Watts area.'

descriptions: Grape Street Crips and Bounty Hunter Bloods signed a truce in 1992.
edges.evidence_quote: a truce that happened between the Grape Street Crips and their rivals, the PJ Watts Crips, as well as both of their rivals, the Hunter and Hacienda Bloods sets

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips is a subset of the Crips gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Grape Street Watts Crips is a set of the Crips gang

descriptions: East Coast Crips murdered a Grape Street Watts Crips member, sparking a war between the two sets.
edges.evidence_quote: Brandon 'BL' Bullard, a veteran of the Grape Street Watts Crips, was murdered by the East Coast Crips in 2008. The murder of Bullard sparked a war that resulted in 26 wounded and 9 dead in the six weeks that followed.

descriptions: Grape Street Crips and Hacienda Village Pirus signed a truce in 1992.
edges.evidence_quote: a truce that happened between the Grape Street Crips and their rivals, the PJ Watts Crips, as well as both of their rivals, the Hunter and Hacienda Bloods sets

descriptions: Grape Street Crips and PJ Watts Crips are feuding.
edges.evidence_quote: In Watts, the Grape Street Crips and the PJ Watts Crips have feuded so much that the PJ Watts Crips even teamed up with a local Blood set, the Bounty Hunter Bloods, to fight the Grape Street Crips.

descriptions: Grape Street Crips and PJ Watts Crips were rivals before the 1992 truce.
edges.evidence_quote: a truce that happened between the Grape Street Crips and their rivals, the PJ Watts Crips

descriptions: Grape Street Crips and PJ Watts Crips signed a truce in 1992.
edges.evidence_quote: a truce that happened between the Grape Street Crips and their rivals, the PJ Watts Crips, as well as both of their rivals, the Hunter and Hacienda Bloods sets

Manson Family.description: The Manson Family was a commune, gang, and cult led by Charles Manson, active in California from the late 1960s to early 1970s. The group consisted of approximately 100 followers who lived an unconventional lifestyle, using psychoactive drugs and radicalized by Manson's teachings. They committed at least nine murders, including the Tate–LaBianca murders, and other crimes. The group dissolved after Manson and other members were arrested.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood had a brief connection with Manson Family, attempted to join forces, but ended due to offense over Tate murder.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, the Aryan Brotherhood had a connection with Charles Manson and the Manson Family. Several members of the Manson Family were in prison at the time, and they attempted to join forces. However, the relationship did not last long as the Aryan Brotherhood took offense at the murder of pregnant actress Sharon Tate.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood had a connection with the Manson Family.
edges.evidence_quote: A principios de la década de 1970, la Hermandad Aria tenía una conexión con Charles Manson y la Familia Manson.

descriptions: Manson found temporary acceptance from the Aryan Brotherhood in prison, but his role was submissive.
edges.evidence_quote: Though he found temporary acceptance from the Aryan Brotherhood , his role was submissive to a sexually aggressive member of the group at San Quentin .

descriptions: Manson Family hired Straight Satans members for security at Spahn Ranch.
edges.evidence_quote: Manson brought in members of the Straight Satans Motorcycle Club to act as security.

descriptions: The Straight Satans had a close association with the Manson Family, with members visiting Spahn Ranch and providing weapons.
edges.evidence_quote: The Straight Satans are perhaps most infamous for their connection to the Manson Family cult.

descriptions: Weathermen praised the Manson Family for the Tate murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Charles Manson was an obsession within the group and Dohrn claimed he truly understood the iniquity of white America, with the Manson family being praised for the murder of Sharon Tate

descriptions: The Mazzara and Caterino-Ferriero clans were in conflict in Cesa.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra il clan Mazzara e il clan Caterino-Ferriero : svoltosi nel comune di Cesa tra il 2005 e il 2009 per il controllo degli affari illeciti nel territorio comunale.

descriptions: Iannazzo and Cannizzaro-Daponte jointly carried out attacks against Torcasio.
edges.evidence_quote: viene ricostruito l'omicidio del presunto boss Antonio Torcasio del 23 maggio 2003 e il tentato omicidio contro Vincenzo Torcasio ad opera degli Iannazzo e dei Cannizzaro-Daponte.

descriptions: Iannazzo is allied with Cannizzaro-Daponte.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata dei Giampà e Cannizzaro-Daponte

descriptions: Iannazzo and Cannizzaro-Daponte jointly attempted a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: il tentato omicidio contro Vincenzo Torcasio ad opera degli Iannazzo e dei Cannizzaro-Daponte

Mongrel Mob.description: The Mongrel Mob is a prominent organised crime group and prison gang based in New Zealand, founded in the 1960s by mainly Pākehā youths in Wellington and Hawke's Bay. It is the largest gang in New Zealand with over thirty chapters, also operating in Australia and Canada. The gang is known for its violent rivalry with Black Power and engages in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, extortion, and other crimes. Members are identified by red and black colors, a bulldog patch, and facial tattoos.

descriptions: Black Power was founded as a response to the Mongrel Mob, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was founded in response to the rival Mongrel Mob gang and white power associated gangs.

descriptions: Bloods and Mongrel Mob are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Guerrilla Family, Black P. Stones, Italian-American Mafia (in New York), Juggalos, Latin Kings, Mongrel Mob, People Nation, United Blood Nation, Vice Lords

descriptions: Blue Angels members forced a Mongrel Mob member to surrender his colors.
edges.evidence_quote: Four Blue Angels members ... forced a member of the Mongrel Mob to hand over his ' colours ' at his home

descriptions: Club Deroes, along with three other clubs, cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: Coffin Cheaters, along with three other clubs, cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: Gods Garbage, along with three other clubs, cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 1971, members of the Hells Angels, Highway 61, the Mongrel Mob and the Polynesian Panthers were involved in a large-scale brawl in Auckland, which resulted in numerous arrests.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bloods , Mongrel Mob , Tribesmen

descriptions: Mongols and Mongrel Mob fought a turf war in New Zealand in 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: In January 2020, the Mongols were involved in a turf war with the Mongrel Mob in the Bay of Plenty, which involved semi-automatic weapons, tit-for-tat shootings and arson attacks.

descriptions: Mongrel Mob and Black Power are primary rivals with a longstanding feud involving violent clashes.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mongrel Mob's primary rival is the Black Power gang, and their longstanding feud has resulted in numerous public and violent clashes over the years.

descriptions: Mongrel Mob and Hells Angels were involved in a brawl together in 1971.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 1971, members of the Hells Angels, Highway 61, the Polynesian Panthers and the Mongrel Mob gangs engaged in a large gang-related brawl in central Auckland.

descriptions: Outback Blacks, a sub-chapter of Black Power, ambushed Mongrel Mob Kawerau chapter in a gunfight.
edges.evidence_quote: On 13 January 2018, a gunfight erupted in Whakatāne, Bay of Plenty, when a funeral procession of the Mongrel Mob Kawerau chapter was ambushed by members of the Outback Blacks – a sub-chapter of Black Power.

descriptions: Tribesmen members fought with Mongrel Mob members in 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2020, 10 members of the Tribesmen Christchurch confronted a small group of Mongrel Mob members outside of the Christchurch District Court.

Angiulo brothers.description: The Angiulo brothers were a leading Italian-American crime group based in Boston's North End, active from the 1960s until the mid-1980s. They were made men in the Patriarca crime family and controlled racketeering throughout Massachusetts. Their headquarters was the 'Dog House' convenience store. The organization was dismantled after FBI bugging, leading to convictions in the 1980s.

descriptions: Charlestown Mob competed with Angiulo brothers for control of rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: until Irish Mob groups such as the Winter Hill Gang and the Charlestown Mob decided to run the rackets in their own neighborhoods

descriptions: Angiulo brothers were members of the Patriarca crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: As "made men" in the Patriarca crime family, they were placed in control of the racketeering throughout Massachusetts

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang competed with Angiulo brothers for control of rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: until Irish Mob groups such as the Winter Hill Gang and the Charlestown Mob decided to run the rackets in their own neighborhoods

Tottenham Mandem.description: Organised street gang based in Tottenham, North London, originating on the Broadwater Farm estate in the 1970s. Involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, armed robbery, kidnapping, and contract killing. Controlled much of the drug markets in North London during the 1990s. Splintered into various groups by the mid-2000s.

descriptions: BWF formed from younger TMD members.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the younger members of TMD had begun to associate with the London Bloods movement and began calling themselves Ida Boys (also known as Ida Rydaz, Ida Bloodstarz, or IDA), Bloodline or Bloodgang, and BWF (Broadwater Farm).

descriptions: Tottenham Mandem is an ally of Ghetto Boys per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Brockley Mandem, Tottenham Mandem , Young Brockley Mandem (YBM)

descriptions: Ida Boys formed from younger TMD members.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the younger members of TMD had begun to associate with the London Bloods movement and began calling themselves Ida Boys (also known as Ida Rydaz, Ida Bloodstarz, or IDA), Bloodline or Bloodgang, and BWF (Broadwater Farm).

descriptions: NPK is a split-off group from TMD.

Ku Klux Klan.description: American white supremacist and far-right hate group, founded in 1865 in Pulaski, Tennessee, by former Confederate soldiers. It has operated in three major iterations, using violence and intimidation to target African Americans, Jews, Catholics, and immigrants. The Klan is considered one of the earliest terrorist groups in the United States, with a history of lynching, political violence, and racial terror. Modern iterations are smaller but continue to promote white nationalism and anti-immigrant nativism.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood lists Ku Klux Klan as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Ku Klux Klan

descriptions: Birger and the Shelton Brothers jointly attacked the Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: Charlie Birger and the Shelton Brothers joined forces to attack the remaining Klan leaders in Herrin, using Tommy guns and shotguns.

descriptions: The Charles Birger gang and Shelton Brothers Gang fought against the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang and rival Charles Birger gang engaged in a war with the Ku Klux Klan that concluded with a shoot out at Herrin.

descriptions: The text states the Grim Reapers have alleged ties to the KKK.
edges.evidence_quote: The Grim Reapers are ... have alleged ties to the Ku Klux Klan (KKK)

descriptions: The IKA claims to be a continuation of the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: The group describes itself as the sixth era of the Ku Klux Klan

descriptions: JDL infiltrated Klan groups to gather intelligence.
edges.evidence_quote: A Jewish Defense League (JDL) member, known by the pseudonym "Annette", infiltrated neo-Nazi and Klan groups in 1979; he informed on two dozen Klansmen and neo-Nazis

descriptions: Klan and National Socialist Party of America jointly attacked communist protesters.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 3, 1979, the Klan and the National Socialist Party of America killed five communist protesters in Greensboro, North Carolina

descriptions: The Red Knights were formed in opposition to the Klan and engaged in violent responses.
edges.evidence_quote: The Red Knights, a militant group formed in opposition to the Klan, responded violently to Klan provocations on several occasions.

edges.evidence_quote: a KKK-related group called Ritter des Feurigen Kreuzes ("Knights of the Fiery Cross") was founded in 1925 in Berlin

descriptions: The True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan is a group associated with the third Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1997, FBI agents arrested four members of the True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan in Dallas for conspiracy to commit robbery and for conspiring to blow up a natural gas processing plant.

descriptions: A Klan leader and a National Socialist Liberation Front leader were arrested together for assaulting a JDL infiltrator.

Five Points Gang.description: The Five Points Gang was a criminal street gang based in the Five Points of Lower Manhattan, New York City, during the late 19th and early 20th century. Founded by Paul Kelly (born Paolo Antonio Vaccarelli), it initially consisted primarily of Irish-Americans but later became predominantly Italian-American. The gang engaged in racketeering, extortion, illegal gambling, and other crimes. Many of its members, including Johnny Torrio, Al Capone, and Lucky Luciano, later became prominent criminals.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang was at war with the Five Points Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s

descriptions: Eastman Gang and Five Points Gang were rivals warring over territory and political work.
edges.evidence_quote: Eventually, the gang became involved in rivalries with other local gangs such as the Yakey Yakes and the Five Points Gang, warring over both territory and work as political sluggers for Tammany Hall.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang competed with the Five Points Gang for control of New York's underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: Monk Eastman operated a powerful Jewish gang known as the Eastman Gang that competed with Italian and Irish gangs , notably Paul Kelly 's Five Points Gang , for control of New York City's underworld.

descriptions: The two gangs fought over territory, engaging in open warfare.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivals disputed claims to a strip of territory of the Lower East Side of Manhattan.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang and Eastman Gang were rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: This was in contrast to many of his fellow Jewish East Siders who predominantly joined the Five Points Gang's rival Eastman Gang led by its namesake Monk Eastman.
